questionText: Are your emissions performance calculations in 7.10 and 7.10.1 based on a location-based Scope 2 emissions figure or a market-based Scope 2 emissions figure?
questionKo: 지역기반 Scope 2 배출량과 시장기반 Scope 2 배출량 중, 귀사가 7.10 및 7.10.1에 입력한 배출량 성과 산정에 반영한 배출량 유형을 선택하십시오.
sectorTags: Financial services, Oil & Gas, Coal, General, Aviation, Capital goods, Transport OEMS - EPM, Chemicals, Metals & mining, Transport services, Steel, Agricultural commodities, Transport OEMS, Energy utilities & power generators, Cement, Food, beverage & tobacco, Paper & forestry, Real estate, Construction
simpleHelp: This question provides more transparency on how your organization's emissions performance figures are derived.

guidance.text: This question only appears if you select "Increased", "Decreased" or "Remained the same overall" in response to 7.10.
guidance.textKo: This 문항 only appears 만약 you select "Increased", "Decreased" 또는 "Remained the same overall" in response to 7.10.

guidance.text:
General 
In alignment with the GHG Protocol Scope 2 Guidance, companies are only required to compare their Scope 2 emissions for either their location-based or market-based figure, but are required to be transparent about which figure they use. 
You should only select one option, as your market-based figure may inherently be a combination of location-based and market-based calculations if you have operations in regions where there are contractual instruments, and other operations in regions where there are not contractual instruments.
guidance.textKo: 일반 In alignment with the GHG Protocol Scope 2 Guidance, companies are only required to compare their Scope 2 배출량 for either their 지역기반 또는 시장기반 figure, but are required to be transparent about which figure they use. You 해야 합니다 only select one option, as your 시장기반 figure may inherently be a combination of 지역기반 및 시장기반 calculations 만약 you have operations in regions 여기서 there are contractual instruments, 및 other operations in regions 여기서 there are not contractual instruments.

criteria.criteria:
Any option selected - 1 point
A maximum of 1/1 point is available for this question.
